safest_plan.entry_basis: zero_hub/zero_tg — the 3M breaker-block 0.5 retrace at $102.50, stacked with the higher-timeframe 0.618 fib, the $100 round-number level and the 2-week demand at $99.50 just below; chart confirms the blue breaker zone and pink $100 marker. Buy zone is really 99.50–102.50.

targets.basis: zero_hub TP1 — matches the ~120 line labelled 4D on the chart
zero_hub TP2 — matches the ~130 line labelled 6D on the chart

safest_plan.stop_basis: Below the 3M demand at $94. Zero's stated invalidation is a 3-month close below the breaker block (~$99.50); that confirms a slide to the $94 demand, so the stop sits beneath $94 to give the swing room rather than at the zone edge.

stance_by_source.summary: Spot buy at the 3-month breaker-block retrace near $102.50, targeting $120 then $130; invalidated on a 3-month close below the breaker block.
Same clean spot setup carried over unchanged — buy the $99.50–$102.50 confluence zone, stop on a close below the breaker block.

plain_english: Every source agrees on the same plan, so the read is consistent: this is a buy-the-dip idea on ConocoPhillips, treated as a long-term spot holding. The plan is to buy in the $99.50–$102.50 area, where several support markers line up, aiming for roughly $120 then $130, and to abandon it if a 3-month candle closes below about $94. The catch is that the stock is currently around $110 — above the buy zone — so nothing should be bought yet. Wait for a pullback into the buy zone before entering.
